hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` card must pin `first_broken_law` to the authoritative primary drift surface, so route-neutral system work cannot legally target a secondary XR gap while the first broken law is still open.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` accepted any `first_broken_law` value that appeared anywhere in `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json.top_drift_surfaces`, so a synchronized falsification that rewrote an XR card from `work.lane.hero.authority` to the secondary surface `work.lane.nonclaims` could still verify clean even though the authoritative first broken law remained open. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any `sysopt.xr-*` card whose `first_broken_law` does not match `target_gap.top_drift_surfaces[0]`, and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if XR systems-optimizer cards must pin `first_broken_law` to `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json.top_drift_surfaces[0]` and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR systems-optimizer ratchets must lock `first_broken_law` to the authoritative primary drift surface; allowing any lower-ranked XR surface keeps route-neutral system work pointed away from the first measured failure.
